title: VCP Task Handle
description: A long-running, grant-bound asynchronous execution handle (SPEC §21). The invocation of a long-running capability returns a task handle instead of a result; status and result are fetched later via tasks/get. A task is a state handle: typed, expiring, subject-scoped. The originating grant governs the whole task lifetime; cancellation revokes the grant.

grant_id.description: The grant that authorizes and bounds this task. The task MUST NOT outlive the grant's expires_at.

subject.description: The owning subject. tasks/get|cancel from a different subject MUST be rejected (SUBJECT_MISMATCH).

status.description: input-required names a signed elicitation schema; the Host resumes by re-issuing with inputResponses.

input_request.description: Present when status=input-required: a signed elicitation schema (§13.2). The Provider MUST NOT smuggle free-form prompt text here.

result_ref.description: Reference to the eventual result+attestation (§9). The result MAY be fetched more than once; result_hash MUST be stable across fetches.

reason_code.description: On status=failed/cancelled, a reason from the §23 registry (e.g. TASK_EXPIRED, GRANT_EXPIRED).
